Transalis Ltd

Backend Software Engineer/Developer (Java Kafka)

Portsmouth, England, United Kingdom · 7 hours ago · 30 applicants

Promoted by hirer · No response insights available yet

Hybrid
Full-time

About the job

About Us:

Transalis is a leading provider of innovative supply chain solutions that enable our customers to reduce operational costs, collect cash quicker and meet their eInvoice mandates requirements. Our technology, support by a great team of people, has made Transalis the trusted partner for companies including, Amazon, AS Watson, Office Depot, Sainsburys Argos and Superdrug.


About the Job:

Are you a Java technologist with Kafka experience seeking a role where you can work on ground breaking technology, be the definitive technical expert and gatekeeper for our product and lead our small Products & Solutions development team?


Whether you are looking to take your career to the next level, or you are a seasoned developer looking to focus deeply on one core product within a small, high-impact team, this role offers the perfect environment. You could be joining a leading B2B integration SaaS service provider.


About You:

  • You have strong Java development skills including multi-threading and concurrency, you confidently enhance, navigate, debug, and maintain core Java-based services.
  • You possess a calm, highly systematic approach to troubleshooting live production emergencies.
  • Experience with Kafka as a messaging platform and own the Kafka cluster management.
  • Act as the technical subject matter expert for live production incident response using centralized logs and metric dashboards.
  • You have a good understanding of AWS and are familiar with DevOps, CI/CD pipelines
  • You're collaborative, enjoy problem solving and interacting with clients
  • You are comfortable taking the reins without needing direction.
  • You are a systems thinker and able to contribute to the overall product roadmap, strategy and development. 



Requirements added by the job poster

• Authorized to work in United Kingdom
